channel.title: Top Level Folder called Content on Unbounded School
channel.description: Recent content in Top Level Folder called Content on Unbounded School

item.description: What I would do with 1,000,000 dollars is buy toys for my sister, nice dresses and earrings for my mom, and Reeses and new shoes for my dad. Then, I would buy lot of beautiful fabric and art materials. Last, I would go all around the world in amazing private plane. That’s just some things I would do with 1,000,000 dollars!

item.title: Fall 2025
item.description:
Fall 2025 Grades

Subject Grade
Math A+
ELA A+
Writing A-
Reading A+
Social Studies A+
Spelling A+
PE A+
Music A-
Extracurricular A+

Key Achievements

Scarlett is making excellent progress toward mastering 4th-grade Math and English, as evidenced by her strong performance in Khan Academy and Lexia. In Math, she has completed more than 50% of the curriculum with 100% mastery. In Lexia, she passed Core 16 with a 95% score and Core 17 with a perfect 100%.

item.title: Schedule
item.description:
Daily

Time Lesson
8:30 Math & Logic
9:45 Recess
10:00 DEAR
10:30 Social Studies
11:15 Lunch
11:30 Recess
12:00 Literacy
1:00 Daily Writing
1:30 Projects
2:15 Music & PE
3:15 End of Day

Math & Logic

Khan Academy is the primary math curriculum. After completing grade-level material, supplement with Beast Academy or Math Academy. Once mastered, advance to the next grade on a limited basis, reducing math to 2–3 days weekly to prioritize Logic, Technology, and Science. For example, replace some math with Learning to Code. Other Math platforms to consider: Matific; Brilliant; Synthesis; Art of Problem Solving

item.title: My Superpower
item.description:
If I had a superpower, I would have the superpower to fly really fast. I would have that power because I think it would be amazing to fly up to the giant clouds and get to places quickly.

I would help people with my superpower by flying up high to look for a little girl’s doll that she lost, or saving a young boy from falling off a high edge. I would help with anything else anyone needed. I am Scarlett the amazing superhero!

item.title: Transcripts
item.description:
Math

Khan Academy Dashboard

Khan Academy Math 4th Grade: 100%

Beast Academy Dashboard

Beast Academy Math Level 4: 40%

Reading & Writing

Reading:

Language:

Lexia Dashboard

Lexia Levels Level 16: 100% Level 17: 100% Level 18: 100%

Khan Academy Dashboard

Khan Academy Literature 4th Grade: 33%
Workbooks 180 Days Language: 58% 180 Days Spelling: 58%

Writing Log

  • Holes
  • 100 Dresses
  • Where The Red Fern Grows
  • Seaside Sanctuary
  • Charlotte's Web
  • Magic Treehouse Twister on Tuesday
  • Little House Big Woods
  • The Scarlet Slipper Mystery
  • Magic Treehouse 4 Books
  • The Hobbit

Science

Workbooks 180 Days Science: 58%

Social Studies

Workbooks 180 Days Geography: 64% 180 Days Social Studies: 58%

Projects & Extracurricular

Scarlett’s writing is a big part of her projects. Reference the Reading & Writing section to view that work.

item.title: Winter 2025
item.description:
Fall 2025 Grades

Subject Grade
Math A+
ELA A+
Writing A+
Reading A+
Social Studies A+
Spelling A+
PE A+
Music -
Extracurricular A+

Key Achievements

Scarlett demonstrated strong mastery of core topics this semester. She fully completed the Khan Academy 4th Grade math curriculum and transitioned to Beast Academy Level 4, earning a 100% on the Khan Academy end-of-year assessment and a MAP Math score of 243 (99th percentile). In Lexia, she completed Level 18 with 98% accuracy and has advanced to Khan Academy 4th Grade Reading units.

item.title: Holes
item.description: In the book Holes there is a boy named Stanley. He went to school and was a pretty big kid, and he got laughed at for that. One day, he was walking under a bridge on his way home from school and from out of the top of the bridge, shoes fell down on him. They were a famous baseball players shoes. The cops came by and took him to court. They gave him the choice to go to jail or Camp Green Lake. He chose Camp Green Lake.

item.title: 100 Dresses
item.description:
The Hundred Dresses, by Eleanor Estes. The theme is forgiveness.

In the book The Hundred Dresses, there is a girl named Wanda. She went to school like any other normal kid, but she had been missing school. In Wanda’s class there was a bunch of kids, but Peggy was the prettiest. Peggy’s best friend was Maddie. They did everything together. Maddie thought she knew why Wanda was missing school. It all started on a beautiful day, Wanda got to school and Peggy asked Wanda how many dresses she had. Wanda only had one dress but she didn’t want to tell everyone that so she said she had one hundred, all lined up in her closet. Everyone started asking her that every day and Maddie could tell that was making Wanda feel uncomfortable. That was why Wanda wouldn’t come to school. Maddie felt bad and so did Peggy.

item.title: Intro To Sewing
item.description: Task: Take a one week sewing tutorial with Mom. On the last day, complete a project by yourself from beginning to end.

item.title: My Awesome Not Cool Dad
item.description:
My Dad is not cool

Sometimes he acts like a fool

He has bad jokes

I hope he doesn’t share’ em with my folks

But my Dad is awesome

Because when he snuggles me I blossom

When he’s near

I feel great cheer

I love him

Even though he needs a trim

He makes me mad

But I love him cause’ he’s my dad

item.title: My Colorado Trip
item.description: My Colorado trip was so much fun! We mostly snowboarded. Snowboarding is not easy, and it takes a while to learn. How do you snowboard? You may ask. The easy way to snowboard is snowplowing. You face the bottom of the hill, and start going down. If you face the right way, you should go pretty slow. Sometimes you have to lift your toes up so the snow doesn’t catch your board. Another way to snowplow is to face the mountain and do the same thing, but lift your heels up instead of your toes.

item.title: One Mile Race
item.description:
Task: Train for, and complete, a 1 mile race. Document what you did along the way.

I trained for a one-mile race once. Every Wednesday, I would practice.

  • On the first Wednesday of practice, I ran two minutes and walked one minute five times.
  • On the second Wednesday, I ran three minutes and walked one minute four times.
  • On the third Wednesday, I ran five minutes and walked one minute two times.
  • The fourth Wednesday I ran a whole mile without stopping to walk.

I ran with some of my neighbors from Crocker Court, so we made shirts that said Crocker Cruisers. It was a blast!

item.title: Where The Red Fern Grows
item.description:
Where the Red Fern Grows, by Wilson Rawls. The theme is working hard to get what you want and loss.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Billy. When Billy was really young he loved to catch things lizards with he hands, when he got a little older, he got little traps to catch little critters. Soon, he got tired of the traps and so desperately wanted two hunting dogs. He asked his parents several times, but they said it was too much money. Billy started losing weight because he wanted two hunting dogs so bad. One night after Billy saw people leave their camp, he saw they had left a newspaper behind.

item.title: A Christmas Story
item.description:
Task: Write a script for a short movie. Direct and film it. Edit it together with Dad in iMovie.

[Scene 1 - in the house by the Christmas tree ]

N - Once upon a time the was two children, Cat and Bun bun, who were playing with blocks on a snowy Christmas Eve.

B - Let’s go play in the snow!

item.title: Thank You Letter
item.description:
Dear Mimi and Paddy,

Thank you so much for letting us stay with you on the other half of our vacation. I loved sleeping in those comfy beds you setup for us. Every night I slept like a baby. Looking for golf balls in your backyard was so much fun. You had so many colorful ones! The food you made us was delicious, especially the tater tots. The presents you got us were awesome! I can’t wait till it rains so I can wear my new purple rain boots! Staying with you was so much fun! I had an amazing time!

item.title: Cupper
item.description: You don’t want to edit this file :-)

item.title: Egg Free Baking
item.description:
Hi there, young baker! Baking is like magic—you mix simple things and turn them into yummy treats. This lesson is all about making egg-free cookies (maybe chocolate chip or something fun like that!), a super easy recipe that’s perfect for beginners. You’ll do most of it yourself, but remember: Always have a grown-up nearby for safety, especially with the oven.

Find a Recipe Online

Baking starts with a good plan! Recipes are like treasure maps that tell you exactly what to do. Today, we’re making egg-free cookies because they’re yummy and safe for everyone—even if someone can’t eat eggs. You get to pick your own recipe this time!

item.title: Grok & Quill
item.description:
Daily Writing

Hey there, writer! Grab your journal and write about your day, a book, or a fun discussion. Make it awesome!

Watch this video, then use one of its 6 cool tips in your writing. You got this!

Grading with Grok

Next, practice typing! Type up your journal entry for some keyboard fun.
